data.title: Unilever Moves Closer to McCormick Deal to Form $60B Food Giant, Stocks Rise
data.description:
Unilever (UL) is reportedly in advanced discussions to merge its food business with spice maker McCormick (MKC) in a deal that could create a combined company valued at around $60 billion. According to sources cited by The Wall Street Journal, the cash-and-stock deal could be announced as early as Tuesday, when McCormick is scheduled to report its latest quarterly results.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

For context, Unilever is a global consumer goods company known for its food, home care, and personal care brands, operating across more than 190 countries. Meanwhile, McCormick is a U.S.-based producer of spices, seasonings, and flavor solutions. On March 20, both companies confirmed they were in discussions regarding a potential strategic transaction involving Unilever's food business.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: General Motors (GM) Extends EV Plant Downtime, Lays Off 1,300 Workers
data.description:
Legacy automaker General Motors (GM) has extended downtime at its Factory ZERO EV plant in Detroit, temporarily laying off about 1,300 workers until April 13. This builds on a pause that began March 16 amid sluggish EV sales. A GM spokesperson explained, "Factory ZERO will temporarily ⁠adjust production to align EV production with ​market demand… Impacted employees may be eligible for sub pay and benefits in accordance with the GM-UAW national contract."

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

GM's move reflects wider industry struggles with EV demand, which has cooled since 2024 peaks due to high prices and charging concerns. Factory ZERO builds high-profile models like the Chevrolet Silverado EV and GMC Hummer EV. Both are facing slower-than-expected uptake despite initial hype.

GM Scales Back EV Investments

GM has recorded $7.6 billion in losses on its EV programs. Like peers, it is cutting back EV plans after new U.S. rules under President Donald Trump, especially the ending of the $7,500 EV tax credit in September 2025. GM has pulled back on EVs before, like canceling the BrightDrop electric delivery vans, switching a Lansing plant to build gas-powered Cadillac CT5 sedans instead of EVs, and dropping plans for EV parts at a Toledo transmission plant.

Factory ZERO has seen prior cuts, including 1,200 jobs in late 2025, over 1,100 in early 2026, and a 50% output reduction in January. These steps reflect a pattern of scaling back ambitious EV goals set years ago.

Shift Toward Gas-Powered Cars

The auto industry has strategically shifted focus to gas-powered trucks and SUVs, which generate most of the profits in Detroit. GM also stated that it will boost heavy-duty truck production at a Michigan plant starting in June. Rival Ford Motor (F) is also ramping up production of gas-powered pickups.

Choosing the best mix of electric and gas-powered vehicles is getting harder, as a war in the Middle East has pushed up gas prices. EV demand has become unpredictable since no one knows how long the war will last or what it will do to gas prices long-term.

Is GM Rated Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On TipRanks, GM has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 15 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell rating. The average General Motors price target of $95.50 implies 31.3% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, GM shares have fallen 10.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Mastercard (MA) Evolves Beyond Card Networks. Here's Why I'm Bullish
data.description:
Mastercard (MA) is often seen as just a card network, even today, but that view is increasingly outdated. The stock has been rangebound over the past year and is down more than 12% year-to-date, compared with a roughly 7% decline in the S&P 500 (SPX). I am bullish on Mastercard because the recent weakness seems disconnected from the company's broader transformation. It is evolving into a platform for payments, software, security, and digital infrastructure, with multiple secular growth drivers that still look underappreciated.

data.title: Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) Stocks Slide Further — Why Analysts Aren't Worried
data.description:
Shares of memory and data storage companies, Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK), fell sharply on Monday, extending their last week's losses. While there was no company-specific news, the decline appears to continue last week's trend, driven by new Google (GOOGL) research that reduces memory requirements for AI models. On Monday, MU stock fell nearly 10%, while SNDK declined 7%. Both stocks dropped another 2% in after-hours trading.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade SNDK

Last week, memory chip stocks tumbled as investors reacted to Google's unveiling of TurboQuant, a new compression algorithm designed to cut memory requirements for AI systems. The announcement sparked concerns that future demand for DRAM and NAND memory may be lower than previously expected, triggering a selloff across the sector. Over the past five days, MU and SNDK have dropped more than 17%.

Why Analysts Aren't Worried

Despite the recent selloff, Wall Street remains largely bullish on memory stocks.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley's five-star analyst Joseph Moore maintained Buy ratings on both Micron and SanDisk, with price targets of $520 and $690, respectively. Moore noted that Google's TurboQuant does not have the effect investors fear, pointing out that the reduction applies only to KV Cache memory, not memory overall. He called it "an evolutionary development, with basically no surprises for memory."

According to Moore, the strength in memory stocks is more durable than the market currently believes.

Additionally, Citrini Research dismissed the sell-off, with an analyst posting on X, "It's like saying Aramco should crash because Toyota came out with a next-generation hybrid engine," implying that the panic over memory demand is unwarranted.

Meanwhile, Travis Prentice, chief investment officer at Informed Momentum Company, said the memory stocks rally "doesn't look like it's over yet, but expectations are high, so it makes sense to take some profits—especially in a troubled market environment."

Which Memory Chip Stock Is the Better Buy, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' Stocks Comparison tool, we compared major memory chip stocks. Among these stocks, MU stock offers the highest upside of 66.7% at a price target of $536.55 with a Strong Buy rating. On the other hand, SNDK offers a modest upside of 22.3%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: CRWV, OKLO, TER: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in CoreWeave and Oklo Stocks, Dumps Teradyne
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made a new shift in the portfolio on Monday, March 30, 2026, cutting exposure to semiconductor testing firm, Teradyne (TER), while adding to AI infrastructure company CoreWeave (CRWV) and nuclear energy firm Oklo (OKLO).

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for CRWV bulls/bears

What Did Wood Sell on March 30?

The most eye-popping move of the day was ARK's massive exit from Teradyne. Wood offloaded 29,130 shares, pocketing roughly $8.6 million.

This continues a recent trend of reducing her position in the chip equipment company as the semiconductor testing market matures. The stock reflected this slowing momentum, closing 7% lower on Monday.

Wood Buys the Dip in CRWV and OKLO

While Teradyne was a clear exit, Wood used a sharp sell-off in CoreWeave as a golden entry point. She poured over $3.1 million into the AI-focused cloud provider just as CRWV stock tumbled nearly 8%.

The slide was triggered by news that CoreWeave's CEO, Michael Intrator, recently sold over $7.2 million worth of company stock. While the "insider selling" headline spooked retail investors, Wood viewed the dip as a great opportunity.

ARK also bought 37,422 shares of Oklo, worth about $1.8 million, buying the dip after the stock fell about 9% on Monday. The move shows Wood's growing focus on nuclear energy, as data centers need large amounts of clean power, with Oklo's small modular reactors seen as a long-term solution.

In healthcare, the firm bought 20,674 shares of GeneDx (WGS) for around $1.18 million, showing continued interest in genomics and precision medicine.

How Wall Street Views These Stocks  

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Futures Dip as Oil Prices Climb Again after Iran Tanker Strike
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved lower on Monday night as oil prices started rising again in late trading. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.61%, 0.26%, and 0.39%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EDT on March 30.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Oil prices jumped after a report from Bloomberg said that Iran struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai waters. Following this news, Brent crude (CM:BZ) rose 2.36% to $115.44 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) gained 3.29% to $106.26 per barrel.

During Monday's regular trading session, stocks were mixed. The S&P 500 fell 0.39%, marking its third straight day of losses. The Nasdaq Composite also declined, dropping 0.73%. However, the Dow managed to edge higher, gaining 49.50 points, or 0.11%.

At the same time, there were signs of possible easing in the Middle East. President Donald Trump said the U.S. is in talks with a "more reasonable" leadership in Iran and noted that progress is being made, raising hopes that the conflict could end soon.

Investors also got some relief from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. He said inflation appears to be under control for now, and there is no immediate need to raise interest rates.

Looking ahead, traders will focus on new economic data on Tuesday. This includes the consumer confidence index for March and the JOLTS job openings report for February.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Stumble as Trump Ramps Iran Threats; Crude Oil Jumps Past $100
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Monday in the red as the U.S.-Iran war entered its fifth week.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • President Trump said the U.S. is in "serious discussions" with Iranian officials to end the war.
  • He also threatened to destroy Kharg Island and Iran's power plants, oil wells, and "possibly all desalinization plants" if Iran refuses to sign a deal and keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed.
  • Rate hike odds dropped after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the Fed is inclined to "look through any kind of a supply shock." The odds of a 25 bps hike by year-end on the CME FedWatch tool fell to 3.8% from 22.2% on Friday.
  • Crude oil futures (CL) rose above $100 per barrel for the first time since the start of the war on reports that Iran's parliament advanced a bill to charge a toll for ships passing through the strait.
  • Aletheia Capital reiterated its "buy" rating and price target of $330 on AMD (AMD). At the same time, Erste Group lowered its rating to "hold" from "buy."
  • SpaceX is considering allocating most of its IPO shares to Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*Trade instead of Robinhood (HOOD) and SoFi (SOFI).
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW) closed higher after its CEO, Nikesh Arora, disclosed a $10 million insider buy.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

The financials group was the best-performing sector on Monday, driven by falling interest rate odds. With lower rates, borrowing becomes cheaper, which can increase demand for loans and support bank lending activity.

Several financial stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Arthur J. Gallagher & Company (AJG)
  • FactSet (FDS)
  • Aon (AON)
  • Marsh & McLennan Companies (MRSH)
  • Brown & Brown (BRO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the industrials sector faced the largest drawdown amid rising oil prices. Higher energy costs increase operating costs for energy-intensive companies, which weighs on profit margins. Energy infrastructure, heavy machinery, and manufacturing firms were hit particularly hard.

Notable industrial stocks trading lower include:

  • Comfort Systems USA (FIX)
  • Generac Holdings (GNRC)
  • Emcor Group (EME)
  • GE Vernova (GEV)
  • Caterpillar (CAT)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Bill Ackman Claims a Fannie Mae & Freddie Mac Stock Surge 'Could Happen Soon'
data.description:
Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA), commonly known as Fannie Mae, and Freddie Mac (FMCC) stocks were a hot topic on Monday following comments from Pershing Square Capital Management founder and CEO Bill Ackman. The hedge fund manager took to X to speak about high-quality businesses that are trading at extremely low prices. When commenting on the mortgage companies, he said, "And Fannie and Freddie are stupidly cheap. Asymmetry at its best. They could be a 10X and it could happen soon."

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Interest in Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac has grown recently due to reports that the U.S. government will hold initial public offerings (IPOs) for the mortgage companies. This would see the return of FNMA and FMCC stocks to public exchanges. Currently, the shares trade over-the-counter after they were delisted from major exchanges in June 2010. This was the result of their being placed in government conservatorship due to the U.S. housing crisis.

An IPO for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac would bring an end to government conservatorship of the companies. However, it's unclear when this would happen. Some sources have claimed the federal government is targeting IPOs for the first half of 2026. Others, such as hedge fund manager Michael Burry, said there's unlikely to be an IPO for these mortgage companies until at least 2027.

Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Stock Movements Today

Fannie Mae stock closed out Friday down 1.62%, while Freddie Mac stock was down 4.78% when markets closed on Friday. The shares haven't performed well recently, with FNMA stock down 54.71% year-to-date and FMCC stock down 56.8% year-to-date. Fannie Mae stock has also fallen 23.1% over the past 12 months, while Freddie Mac stock has dropped 18.13% over that same period of time.
data.author: William White

data.title: FANG and CRGY: Investors Pick 2 Oil Stocks to Buy as Crude Prices Rise
data.description:
Depending on who you listen to – the media, the commentary you follow, or the latest messaging out of the Trump White House – the drama in the Middle East is either intensifying in scope or about to wind down.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

One thing is certain – the world's oil markets are feeling the strain. Prices have pushed higher, with WTI crude trading around $106 and Brent hovering near $112, as the conflict continues to ripple through both futures markets and the physical trade. The surge comes as it becomes clear that Iran is playing its strongest card by clamping down on the Strait of Hormuz for all its worth.

With some 20% of the world's oil traversing this narrow waterway, it's no surprise that oil markets are in flux. Not even the release of 400 million barrels of oil from strategic reserves and temporarily lifting sanctions on banned Russian and Iranian oil have succeeded in calming jittery markets.

While the world waits as the Trump administration weighs its options, some investors are looking for ways to survive the tumult by focusing on those energy stocks best positioned to capitalize on the changing dynamic.

That's where investor Lee Samaha sees opportunity. He's bullish on Diamondback Energy (NASDAQ:FANG), a Permian Basin-focused producer operating across West Texas and New Mexico, right at the core of America's most productive oil region.

In 2025, Diamondback pumped an average of 497,000 barrels of oil per day, with output climbing to 512,800 barrels per day in the fourth quarter. Just as important, it's translating that production into shareholder returns – generating $1.2 billion in adjusted free cash flow and returning 62% of that, or $734 million, through buybacks and dividends.

"A higher oil price obviously helps Diamondback," states the 5-star investor, who is among the top 4% of stock pros covered by TipRanks. Beyond the obvious pressure point of Hormuz, Samaha adds that the war has the potential to significantly set back production capabilities throughout the Gulf region.

"The pressure could be more sustained if there's greater damage to energy infrastructure in the region," adds Samaha. (To watch Lee Samaha's track record, click here)

Wall Street is feeling pretty good about FANG as well. With 21 Buys and 4 Holds, FANG enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. (See FANG stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Oil Pushes Past $100 as Iran Advances Hormuz Toll Plan
data.description:
Crude oil futures (CL) climbed past $100 per barrel on Monday for the first time since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28 as the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill. In addition, the Majlis, Iran's parliament, is working on a bill to charge vessels passing through the passageway.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears

"Security and services for ships will be ensured through fees [for passage through the strait] stipulated by the bill," said Alaeddin Boroujerdi, a member of the Majlis' Committee for Foreign Policy and National Security.

U.S. Opposes Hormuz Toll as Passage Remains Closed

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said the U.S. opposes the toll as the war enters its fifth week. Earlier today, President Trump warned that the U.S. would destroy key Iranian energy infrastructure if the strait remains closed and if Iran refuses to sign a deal.

The passageway is responsible for roughly 20% of global oil flows, while its closure has created an oil supply crunch, sending prices higher.
data.author: Eddie Pan
